tasks.name: CI Workflow + Auto-Merge Gate
tasks.description:
**Task 00 — CI Workflow + Auto-Merge Gate**

_Phase 0 · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2_

**Objective**

Establish the GitHub Actions CI that gates every task PR and enables safe auto-merge, tuned for an Electron + Node bridge + Python voice monorepo.

**Files in scope**

- GitHub Actions workflow: install, lint, typecheck, and `npm run build` for the Electron/renderer; a Node job for the bridge; a lightweight Python lint/import job for the voice package (skippable until Phase 7 code exists).
- Branch protection on main: PRs require the CI check to pass before merge; configure auto-merge so a green PR merges without manual click.
- Fail fast + clear logs; jobs matrixed by workspace (hud / bridge / voice).
- Document the CI contract in README-DEV.md so every subsequent task knows what 'green' means.

**Acceptance criteria**

- A trivial PR against main is BLOCKED until CI passes, then auto-merges when green.
- CI runs lint + typecheck + build for the HUD, and a bridge job; the voice job no-ops gracefully when there is no Python code yet.
- Branch protection verified active on main.

---
📄 [Full prompt fed to the build agent →](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2/blob/main/build/prompts/task-00.md)
tasks.promptSummary:

tasks.name: Vera Core-Integrity Gate (additive/reversible proof)
tasks.description:
**Task 35 — Vera Core-Integrity Gate (additive/reversible proof)**

_Phase 4 · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2_

**Objective**

Prove — not merely assert — that installing the War Room tool/skill leaves Vera's core untouched and is cleanly reversible.

**Files in scope**

- Capture a BEFORE snapshot of Vera's install on the Mac mini: agent config, prompt/identity files, skill inventory, tool registry, and file hashes/manifest of the core install dir.
- Install the war-room skill + warroom_emit tool (additive), then capture an AFTER snapshot.
- Diff: the ONLY differences permitted are the added skill file + the added tool registration. Any change to core prompt/identity/config/other-skills FAILS the gate.
- Then run the reversal (remove skill + unregister tool) and capture a THIRD snapshot; it must match the BEFORE snapshot exactly.
- Emit a signed integrity report (before/after/reverted manifests + diff) committed to the repo.

**Acceptance criteria**

- Before->after diff shows ONLY the additive skill + tool; nothing else changed.
- After->reverted returns byte-for-byte to the before state.
- Integrity report committed; this gate blocks Phase 4 completion.

---
📄 [Full prompt fed to the build agent →](https://github.com/jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2/blob/main/build/prompts/task-35.md)
tasks.promptSummary:
**Vera Core-Integrity Gate (additive/reversible proof)**

**Done when:**

tasks.description:
**task-45 — Re-target the warroom-emit installer to the OpenClaw 2026.5.x plugin registry (RND-65)**

**Objective**

Replace the legacy `plugins.load.paths` registration with a registry-based
install/uninstall that actually loads the plugin on OpenClaw 2026.5.x, while
keeping the install strictly **additive and byte-exactly reversible**.

**Requirements**

1. **Installer (`vera/integrity/src/install.ts`)**
   - Keep copying the skill payload (`skills/war-room`) and the tool payload
     (`tools/warroom-emit`) into the OpenClaw home exactly as today.
   - Replace the `addPluginPath(cfg, registrationPath)` config edit with a
     registry install. Prefer invoking the real CLI:
     `openclaw plugins install <toolDist-or-manifest-dir> --link` then
     `openclaw plugins enable warroom-emit`. Resolve the `openclaw` binary
     robustly (allow an `OPENCLAW_BIN` override; default to `openclaw` on PATH).
   - Capture an install **receipt** that records exactly what was changed so
     uninstall is deterministic: the plugin id (`warroom-emit`), the installed
     path, whether the plugin was already present before (so uninstall does not
     remove a pre-existing one), and the pre-install `openclaw.json` bytes +
     sha256 (the registry/enable step DOES write config — stash the original
     bytes just like today so the reversal can verify).
   - Do NOT hand-edit `plugins.load.paths` anymore.
2. **Uninstaller (`uninstallWarroom`)**
   - Reverse via the CLI: `openclaw plugins disable warroom-emit` +
     `openclaw plugins uninstall warroom-emit` (guard with the receipt so a
     pre-existing plugin is never removed), then remove the copied skill/tool
     payloads and the stash, and verify the resulting `openclaw.json` matches
     the stashed original **modulo the registry/enable entry** (documented,
     structural comparison — mirror the current refuse-to-clobber safety).
   - Preserve the existing safety: refuse to proceed if the …

**Acceptance criteria**

- Unit tests for the new install/uninstall receipt logic and config-delta
  registry helpers.
- An integration-style test that, given a fake OpenClaw home + a stub
  `openclaw` CLI (or the real one if available in CI), proves: install →
  `warroom-emit` present & enabled in the registry/config → uninstall →
  config byte-identical to the pre-install stash and payloads removed.
- Do not weaken existing tests. `npm run build` + `npm test` must be green.

**Out of scope**

- The task-35 integrity GATE re-work and volatile-state scoping (that is
  task-46). This task only re-targets install/uninstall + delta; task-46
  updates the gate to evaluate the new install path and fix RND-64.

**task-46 — Rework the task-35 core-integrity gate for the registry install + fix volatile-state scoping (RND-64)**

**Objective**

Make the integrity gate produce a **stable, meaningful invariant**: a BEFORE ==
REVERTED proof that holds across restarts and correctly reflects the registry
install, so "War Room install is additive + byte-exactly reversible" is provable

**Requirements**

1. **Narrow the coreSet (`core-set.ts`)** to behavior-defining inputs only:

     `identity/device-auth.json`, logs, caches, `.telegram-sent-messages.json`,
     and anything else that mutates on a normal restart. Prefer an explicit
     allowlist of behavior files + a denylist of known-volatile patterns; do not

     `docs/VERA-CORE-INTEGRITY.md`.
2. **Update the gate cycle (`gate.ts`, `cli.ts`)** to use the task-45

3. **Restart-stability test:** add a test proving the coreSet digest is
   IDENTICAL across a simulated normal restart (mutating only volatile files) —
   i.e. volatile churn must not move the digest.
4. **Full-cycle gate test:** before → registry install → after → uninstall →
   reverted; assert `before.digest === reverted.digest` AND the only allowed
   intermediate config delta is the registry-enable entry. Emit the SIGNED
   report; `verify-report` still passes.

**Acceptance criteria**

- Updated full-cycle gate test green against the task-45 install path.

tasks.name: Bridge handshake declares operator role + operator.write scope (RND-66b)
tasks.description:
**task-48 — Bridge gateway handshake must declare operator role + operator.write scope (RND-66 part B)**

**Objective**

The bridge must authenticate AS an operator with the scopes required to drive
the HUD (at minimum `operator.write`, plus `operator.read` for the activity
subscription it already uses), so emits succeed on a token-auth gateway.

**Requirements**

1. In `gateway-session.ts`, extend the connect handshake params to declare:
   - `role: "operator"`
   - `scopes: ["operator.read", "operator.write"]` (make the scope list a
     typed constant; do not request `operator.admin`/`operator.pairing` —
     least privilege: only what emit + activity subscription need).
2. Keep the existing protocol range + `auth.token` behavior unchanged; the
   scopes/role are additive to the same handshake frame.
3. If the gateway still answers with an unauthorized/scope failure, classify it
   with a CLEAR, distinct error (extend `HandshakeFailureKind` if useful, e.g.
   `insufficient-scope`) and log the requested vs granted scopes so a future
   operator sees exactly what to fix — no silent generic backoff for a scope
   denial.
4. Do NOT change the token staging mechanism
   (`~/.openclaw/secrets/warroom/gateway.json` / `WARROOM_GATEWAY_TOKEN`) — that
   is proven working. This is purely the declared role/scopes in the handshake.

**Acceptance criteria**

- Unit test asserting the emitted connect frame includes
  `role: "operator"` and `scopes` containing `operator.write` and
  `operator.read`.
- Test the new scope-denial classification path (given a simulated gateway
  scope-refusal frame, the session surfaces the distinct failure + logs
  requested/granted scopes) without crashing.
- `npm run build` + `npm test` green; do not weaken existing gateway-session
  handshake/backoff tests.

tasks.name: warroom_emit reliably enters agent toolset when bridge reachable (RND-66a)
tasks.description:
**task-49 — Make the warroom_emit tool reliably enter the agent toolset when the bridge is reachable (RND-66 part A)**

**Objective**

When the bridge is reachable, `warroom_emit` MUST be present in Vera's toolset
on the very next agent turn — deterministically, not racily — while preserving
the safety property that a DOWN bridge does not break Vera (the tool may be
present but must nack cleanly, OR be absent; pick the approach that is both
reliable on 2026.5.2 AND safe).

**Requirements**

Choose the most robust approach for 2026.5.2 (justify in the PR), likely:
1. **Register the tool statically** (always present in the toolset) and move the
   reachability decision to **execute-time**: if the bridge is unreachable when
   a command is emitted, return the existing `ERR_HUD_UNAVAILABLE` nack instead
   of throwing — Vera stays unharmed, and the tool never mysteriously vanishes.
   This removes the per-turn null-return entirely. -- OR --
2. If a dynamic factory must be kept, make reachability **synchronously
   readable and fresh**: maintain a cached `reachable` refreshed by a
   background interval (e.g. a short timer that probes `/health` and updates the
   cached flag), so the factory reads an up-to-date value without awaiting
   inside the factory. Ensure the interval is unref'd and cleaned up.
   (Option 1 is preferred for determinism; use option 2 only if static
   registration is not compatible with the desired UX.)
3. Preserve: bridge-dies-mid-turn safety (execute nacks, never throws into
   Vera's turn); the origin stamping (`originFromToolContext`); and the
   optional/`toolMetadata` shape.
4. Keep the plugin ADDITIVE and inert-when-down in spirit: a down bridge must
   never degrade Vera's normal behavior.

**Acceptance criteria**

- Test proving that with a reachable bridge stub, the registered tool is
  present/usable on the first evaluation (no dependence on a prior settled
  probe) — i.e. the race is gone.
- Test proving that with the bridge DOWN, emitting returns the
  `ERR_HUD_UNAVAILABLE` nack (option 1) or the tool is cleanly absent (option
  2), and in NEITHER case does Vera's turn throw.
- `npm run build` + `npm test` green; do not weaken existing tool tests.

---

tasks.name: Per-turn thinking control for voice + HUD - fast by default, deep on demand (RND-89)
tasks.description:
**Task 69 — Per-turn thinking control for voice + HUD (fast by default, deep on demand) (RND-89)**

**Objective**

1. **Fast by default on voice.** The voice session should default to **no
   extended thinking** (`thinking: "off"` or `"minimal"`) so short PTT turns are
   snappy. This is the biggest immediate latency win and should be the default
   voice behavior.
2. **Deep on demand, per turn.** A **voice command** ("Vera, think hard about
   this…" / "deep think") and a **HUD button/hotkey** flip the *next* turn (or a
   sticky mode) to a high thinking level (`high`/`max`), then revert to fast.
3. Telegram/EA/cron behavior unchanged (only the War Room voice path is
   affected).

**Files in scope**

- `bridge/src/gateway-session.ts` — add `thinking` to the voice pin / voice
  `agent` turn; per-turn override plumbing.
- `bridge/src/config.ts` — `WARROOM_VOICE_THINKING` (default fast) +
  deep-level config.
- `bridge/src/hud-server.ts` / `router.ts` + `packages/protocol` — `thinking.mode`
  HUD event schema + routing.
- `src/` renderer — Fast/Deep toggle button + hotkey + indicator.
- `voice/warroom_voice/` and/or the war-room skill — spoken deep-think phrase →
  per-turn thinking override.
- Tests alongside each.

**Acceptance criteria**

1. **Unit:** the voice pin/turn carries the configured `thinking` level;
   default is fast (`off`/`minimal`); a per-turn override sets the requested
   level and reverts. HUD `thinking.mode` event validated + routed. Telegram
   path unchanged.
2. **Bridge suite** green (baseline preserved) + new tests. lint/typecheck/build
   green (protocol, bridge, warroom-emit, renderer as touched). Python voice
   tests for the spoken-toggle path if implemented pipeline-side.
3. **Live (Vera's Mac mini):**
   - A short PTT turn on default mode is **noticeably faster** than today's
     adaptive-thinking latency (measure first-token; compare to the ~3s Task-66
     short-turn baseline — should be at/under it, and the "big turn ~15s
     outlier" should shrink).
   - Saying the deep-think phrase (or pressing the HUD Deep button) makes the
     next answer visibly more considered (higher thinking), then reverts.
   - Telegram answer unaffected.

**Reference**

- Confirm from docs whether per-request `thinking` on the `agent` method is
  operator-allowed (unlike per-request `model`, which is admin-gated). If it is
  NOT, set the level via the session default on `sessions.create` and flip the
  session for deep mode. Prefer the least-privilege path.
- The immediate default-fast change (A) is the highest-value latency win — make
  sure that lands even if B/C are scoped tighter.
- Levels reference: `off | minimal | low | medium | high | xhigh | adaptive |
  max`. Claude 4.6 defaults to `adaptive` when unset — that is the bug this
  fixes.

tasks.name: Voice replies take the briefing stage then restore prior layout (RND-90)
tasks.description:
**Task 70 — Voice replies take the "briefing" stage, then restore the prior layout (RND-90)**

**Objective**

When **Vera answers a voice (PTT) turn**, the HUD should present her the way the
briefing does — switch to the **briefing preset layout** (orb center-stage) for
the duration of her spoken reply — and then **automatically restore the layout
that was showing before she started speaking** once she finishes.

Behavior, in one line: *voice reply begins → snapshot current layout + apply
briefing preset → Vera speaks → reply ends (or barge-in) → restore the
snapshotted layout.*

This is a general "Vera takes the stage to speak" behavior for **ad-hoc voice
replies**, distinct from the scripted morning briefing (which already manages
its own preset + handoff).

**Files in scope**

- `bridge/src/voice-reply.ts` — capture-on-speaking-start, restore-on-idle/final/
  barge-in; once-per-run guard; briefing-active guard.
- `bridge/src/binder.ts` (or wherever `snapshot()` lives) — expose enough state
  to reproduce the prior layout (may already suffice).
- `bridge/src/gateway-session.ts` / bridge state — a "briefing active" flag set
  around `runBriefing`, if that is the chosen guard signal.
- `bridge/src/config.ts` — `WARROOM_VOICE_STAGE` (default on).
- Tests alongside.

**Acceptance criteria**

1. **Unit:** on a voice reply run, the first speaking transition triggers exactly
   one snapshot + briefing-preset apply; the terminal transition triggers exactly
   one restore to the snapshotted layout. Barge-in coalesces (no flicker;
   new turn re-stages). A briefing-active run **skips** stage/unstage. Text-less
   tool-only turn restores cleanly (or never staged).
2. **Bridge suite** green (baseline preserved) + new voice-reply layout tests.
   lint/typecheck/build green.
3. **Live (Vera's Mac mini):** with the HUD on ops-focus, press PTT and ask Vera
   a normal question → the wall switches to the **briefing preset (orb
   center-stage)** while she speaks, then **returns to ops-focus** (the exact
   prior layout) when she finishes. Interrupt mid-reply → the new turn stages
   cleanly. Run the scripted briefing → it still does its own preset + handoff,
   with **no** double-apply or premature restore.

**Reference**

- Fire stage-in on the **first** speaking transition per run only — the streamed
  path emits `orb.state: speaking` on the first chunk; do not re-apply the preset
  on every chunk.
- Restore on **every** terminal path (final, no-reply reset, barge-in) — the orb
  already settles to idle on all of them; add the layout restore alongside.
- The briefing-active guard is the highest-risk correctness item — verify the
  scripted briefing is untouched (its handoff to ops-focus must remain the last
  word).
- Keep everything best-effort; never let a layout op break Vera's reply.

tasks.description:
**task-79 — Global TIME-RANGE control: TODAY | MTD | YTD (RND-103)**

**Objective**

1. **Top-bar segmented control** — `TODAY | MTD | YTD` in the HUD chrome; reflects current range; keyboard + click.
2. **Protocol command `range.set(range: today|mtd|ytd)`** — additive HUD-PROTOCOL v1 event; bridge injects the range
   into range-aware view queries and re-pushes `panel.update` for affected panels.
3. **Voice intents** — "show me year to date" / "month to date" / "switch to today" (and natural variants) map to
   `range.set`; Vera confirms briefly.
4. **Range-aware views re-query** to the selected scope (signed counts, leaderboards, conversion, lockdown/drop rate,
   productivity trend, ratings distribution, office/rep CV, referral-out, insurance mix, recruitment funnel).
5. **Point-in-time / LIVE views IGNORE the range** and render a `LIVE / NOW` tag: `team-presence`,
   `retention-request-queue`, `intake-case-inventory`, `clock-brand`, `vera-orb`, fleet/exec health.
6. **In-view mini-context** — where one headline numb …

**Files in scope**

- `hud/src/renderer/src/` — top-bar range control component + wiring into `App.tsx` chrome; range state in the store.
- `protocol/src/events.ts` — add `range.set` (additive, backward-compatible).
- `bridge/src/router.ts` + `bridge/src/registry.ts` — accept `range.set`, inject range into range-aware query params
  (`?month=` / date args / a new `?from=`/`year` for YTD where verified/added in task-78), re-push affected panels.
- `voice/` — range intents mapped to `range.set`.
- Preset persistence layer — store the range.

**Acceptance criteria**

1. Top-bar TODAY|MTD|YTD control changes the wall's range and all range-aware panels re-query + re-render.
2. `range.set` is a valid additive protocol command; round-trips HUD ↔ bridge.
3. Voice "year to date" / "month to date" / "today" trigger `range.set` with brief confirmation.
4. LIVE/point-in-time views ignore the range and show a LIVE/NOW tag (verified for at least `team-presence`,
   `retention-request-queue`, `intake-case-inventory`, `clock-brand`).
5. `signed-counter-hero` shows the headline + the other ranges as secondary mini-context figures.
6. Range persists in the saved preset and restores on load.
7. `npm run build` + tests green; CI green on `main`.

**Reference**

- `docs/HUD-VIEW-CATALOG.md` → GLOBAL TIME-RANGE CONTROL section; §5 endpoint-implication note (YTD may need a
  `?from=`/`year` param).
- `ROUND-13-BUILD-PACKAGE.md` §6 (task-79).

**Task 85 — Point Calendar at Zeri's calendar (PM-1 Q2 fix / RND-114)**

**Objective**

task-84 shipped the Calendar stage but defaults to `calendarId = 'primary'` using
Virgil's Google token (`~/.openclaw/secrets/google-tokens.json`). **Design-spec Q2
requires Zeri's calendar** ("Zeri's calendar is the goal, not Jon's/Virgil's").
This task closes that gap. Read-only stays (Q1).

**Files in scope**

- Make the calendar **source configurable and default to Zeri's calendar**:
  - Accept a `WARROOM_CALENDAR_ID` (and token path) so the served day is Zeri's.
  - Two supported wirings, pick per infra reality at build time:
    (a) Virgil's token with Zeri's calendar **shared to** virgil@ and
        `calendarId = <zeri's calendar id>`, OR
    (b) a dedicated **Zeri Google token** file + `calendarId = 'primary'`.
  - Recommend (a) if Zeri shares his calendar to virgil@ (no new OAuth);
    fall back to (b) if a Zeri token is provisioned.
- **GATE:** live Zeri data depends on Zeri's Google authorization/sharing being
  resolved (the ⚠️ open action from 2026-07-13). If not yet available at build
  time, wire the config + default + tests against a fixture and clearly gate the
  live switch on the credential; do NOT hardcode Virgil's calendar as the answer.
- Keep the existing offline fallback …

**Acceptance criteria**

  error state) reusable by the search view and later proactive panels.

**Acceptance criteria**

  with clean searching→done→error states.
- Debounce/rate-limit + a hard rule that it never overrides briefing/stage/fullscreen.
- Explicit entry runs a real query end-to-end (mocked provider in test).
- Tests cover streaming assembly, citation shape, and coexistence guard (D-2).

- No stuck mic on stop/failure; recording state is observable.

**Acceptance criteria**

- With assist mode ON, an on-topic whitelisted phrase (e.g. "how's intake this week")
  produces a correct decision object above threshold.
- Off-whitelist / below-threshold input produces NO decision (quiet).
- Assist mode OFF → classifier inert.
- Tests cover whitelist match, threshold behavior, and opt-in gating (D-2).

**Reference**

`docs/POST-MVP-DESIGN-SPEC.md` §4 + Q6/Q7, task-90 pipeline, task-86 registry,

  chart/panel via the existing view registry + Substrate C streaming contract.
- **Guardrails (critical, spec §4.1):**
  - **Only touch "free" side panels** — NEVER override the briefing, staging, a
    full-screen takeover (calendar/search/meeting/KB), or a panel the user is actively using.
  - **Debounce / rate-limit** so it does not thrash panels on every utterance; cooldown
    between emits.
  - **Manual mute** ("stop showing me stuff" / assist-mode off via task-86 registry) —
    instant, and a "was this useful?" feedback affordance to tune (Q optional).
- **Restore:** proactive panels are transient; they yield immediately if the user
  starts a briefing/stage/fullscreen.

**Acceptance criteria**

- An above-threshold decision surfaces the right panel into a free slot without a command.
- It NEVER overrides briefing/stage/fullscreen/active panel (hard test).
- Debounce/cooldown prevents thrash; mute ("stop showing me stuff") silences it instantly.
- Turning assist mode off (task-86) stops all proactive emits.
- Tests cover emit, the never-override guard, debounce, and mute (D-2).

**Reference**

`docs/POST-MVP-DESIGN-SPEC.md` §4, task-97 decision object, task-90 streaming contract,
view registry (`hud/src/renderer/src/views/`), briefing/stage coexistence rules.

tasks.name: PG-b: Test Mode arming + harness core, real-pipeline injection, 3-attempt/6-exchange loop (RND-127)
tasks.description:
**Task 100 — PG-b: Test Mode arming + harness core (RND-127)**

**Objective**

The engine of Proving Ground: arm Test Mode (Jon-only), and drive catalog items through the
REAL pipeline with a bounded attempt loop and a verdict engine. Entering Test Mode pauses the
automated run so Jon can take the wheel.

**Files in scope**

- **Test Mode arm flag:** armed ONLY on Jon's explicit command (Jon-authenticated — his
  Telegram id / an explicit armed token). NEVER self-activates. NEVER available to Zeri or any
  office user. Off = zero footprint.
- **PROPOSE the exact arm/disarm gesture in this task BEFORE building** (a Jon-only command that
  toggles armed state + pause/resume), designed to match user intent (spec §I). Document the
  chosen mechanism.
- **Harness core:** inject each catalog item's `command` through the REAL turn path
  (`voice/warroom_voice/cli.py:cmd_turn` or the bridge turn endpoint) — NOT a mock. Capture the
  HUD render + Vera reply.
- **Loop (PER ITEM):** up to 3 attempts = 6 exchanges (attempt = 1 inject-in + 1 response-out).
  If `expected` unmet, rephrase/retry ≤3, then verdict FAIL/needs-fix and move on.
- **Verdict engine:** PASS / PARTIAL / FAIL / BLOCKED / NOT-RUN per item.
- **Pause …

**Acceptance criteria**

- Test Mode cannot arm except on Jon's authenticated command (hard test).
- Harness injects a catalog item through the real pipeline and records a verdict.
- 3-attempt/6-exchange loop enforced per item; FAIL after 3 failed attempts.
- Entering Test Mode pauses an active run; exit resumes.
- Mutations are dry-run unless per-item opt-in. No regression when Test Mode off (D-1).
- Tests cover arming auth, injection, the loop bound, verdicts, pause/resume, dry-run (D-2).

**Reference**

`build/proving-ground/DESIGN-SPEC.md` §B, §D, §I. Turn path (`voice/warroom_voice/cli.py`),
bridge turn endpoint, C6 confirm-gate (`bridge/src/confirm-gate.ts`).

tasks.name: PG-d: Run logging + summary -> next-round packager (RND-129)
tasks.description:
**Task 102 — PG-d: Run logging + summary → next-round packager (RND-129)**

**Objective**

Every Proving Ground run must produce a durable, reviewable record and then convert results into
draft instructions for the next round — following the test-then-plan rule (organize phases only
AFTER the full result inventory exists).

**Files in scope**

- **Per-run log:** `build/proving-ground/runs/run-<ISO>.json` plus a human-readable `.md` mirror.
  Per scored item: injected input(s), responses, attempt history, verdict, free-text observation,
  recommendation, timestamps.
- **End-of-run packager:** compile the FULL result inventory, auto-summarize (counts by verdict,
  by testingArea, notable failures), and emit DRAFT next-round/phase instructions.
- **test-then-plan:** the packager organizes proposed rounds/phases only from the actual inventory,
  never from a pre-baked plan.

**Acceptance criteria**

- A completed run writes a valid `run-<ISO>.json` + `.md` with all required per-item fields.
- The packager produces a summary + draft next-round instructions derived from the inventory.
- Machine-parseable verdicts so the summary auto-compiles.
- Tests cover log write, schema, and summary/packager output (D-2).

**Reference**

`build/proving-ground/DESIGN-SPEC.md` §E, §G.

   settle at each inter-utterance gap).

   forever — reuse/extend the caption engine's timeout bound rather than inventing a second unrelated
   timer.
4. On barge-in / interrupt, the orb settles promptly (interrupted === true).

**Acceptance criteria**

- The orb remains in its speaking animation for the full duration Vera is audibly speaking (single- and
  multi-utterance replies); it no longer settles early.
- On a genuine end (`speak.done`) or interrupt, the orb settles within the existing readability/timeout
  window — never stuck.
- Caption behavior is unchanged (captions already correct; do not regress their timing).

tasks.promptSummary:
**RND-174: Quiesce the panel poller while a proving run is live (freeze ROOT CAUSE)**

**Why:** The mid-run control freeze Jon hit is caused by the shared bridge's `PanelPoller` refreshing 9 dashboard panels on the same event loop as the operator's STEP/HALT/Next control traffic during a proving run. Over a long all-77 STEP run this…

**Do:**
- When `provingGround.run.state` transitions to `running`, PAUSE `PanelPoller` (stop panel refresh ticks).
- Resume the poller on run end (`done` / `stopped`). Idempotent + restart-safe (mirror registry reschedule-on-attach semantics).
- Keep the task-148 analyst timeout + circuit breaker as defense-in-depth.

**Done when:**
- During a live all-77 STEP run: ZERO panel-refresh traffic on the bridge; STEP/HALT/Next respond <500ms at every boundary across the full ru…
- Unit test: poller paused on run-state=running, resumed on done/stopped; safe if run never starts.
- Gates green.

tasks.name: RND-175: per-item settle watchdog — a hung Vera turn can never freeze the run again
tasks.description: RND-175: per-item settle watchdog — a hung Vera turn can never freeze the run again
tasks.promptSummary:
**RND-175: Per-item settle watchdog (a hung Vera turn can never freeze the run)**

**Why:** The STEP loop (test-harness.ts run() ~L1115, stepGate ~L1099) waits on exchange boundaries with NO item-level watchdog. If Vera hangs mid-item (gateway stall, model timeout), the item never settles and the run never parks — the operator is…

**Do:**
- Give each item a settle watchdog (configurable, default ~90s from item injection).
- If an item does not settle before the watchdog: force-settle it with verdict `HALTED` (or `FAIL` per spec §D) and note `watchdog: no settle in Ns`, t…
- A slow-but-completing item must NOT be falsely halted (reset watchdog on observed progress/exchange).

**Done when:**
- Simulate a non-settling item → run auto-settles it (HALTED/FAIL w/ watchdog note), parks, operator continues via Next.
- Watchdog is per-item; a slow-but-completing item still passes normally.
- Run never hangs with frozen controls. Gates green.

tasks.name: RND-A: stage rehydration on HUD reconnect + consent invariant
tasks.description: **Audit D-1 / XC-1 (High).** On HUD reconnect the bridge re-hydrates only the DEFAULT layout (`binder.ts:83`); KB/Search/Meeting/wrap-up directors keep `staged` in memory but never re-assert, so a refresh blanks the wall. In a live meeting the mic keeps recording against a blank wall — a consent-invariant break.
tasks.doneWhen: HUD reload mid-KB/Search/Meeting returns to the exact staged view within one reconnect; mid-meeting reload restores the recording indicator (consent invariant holds).
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
HUD reconnect restores only the default layout; open stages vanish. Live meeting reload = mic records against blank wall (consent break).
## Build
Add a bridge-side `rehydrate(client)` hook on `client-connected`: every director with `staged !== null` re-asserts `layout.apply`+`panel.set`+last `panel.update`; meeting re-asserts the recording indicator; search re-pushes `lastResult`.
## Acceptance
Reload mid-KB/Search/Meeting returns exact view; recording indicator restored; no double-stage; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-B: fold visual verdict into proving-ground verdict (kills false PASS)
tasks.description: **Audit D-2 (High).** Proving-ground PASS/FAIL comes purely from the text turn (`test-harness.ts:1504`); the vision grade is attached (`:1512`) but never folds in — a visually-broken wall still reports MET. This is the root of the 'false PASS' class.
tasks.doneWhen: A text-passing visualSmoke item with a broken/blank screenshot no longer reports MET — it reports FAIL (or partial) with a visual-downgrade reason.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
Visual verdict is advisory only; a fail-graded (overlap/blank) screen still reports MET.
## Build
For visualSmoke items, fold `visualVerdict==='fail'` into a met→FAIL (or partial) downgrade; skipped/partial stay advisory; surface the downgrade reason in run JSON/MD.
## Acceptance
Text-pass + broken screenshot = FAIL with reason; clean screenshot unchanged; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-C: authoritative DELIVER hold resolution (button/voice parity)
tasks.description: **Audit D-3 / MTG-1,2 (High).** The meeting DELIVER button re-arms only via a 120s local TTL (`deliverHoldStore.ts:14`); the voice confirm-gate resolves with no HUD signal (`confirm-gate.ts:85`). Spoken deliver never sets the flag, so the button stays enabled and the next click is refused.
tasks.doneWhen: DELIVER button un-holds the instant the confirm-gate resolves; spoken hold disables the button; no click is silently gate-refused.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
DELIVER button lies up to 120s; spoken vs button deliver diverge on the HUD flag.
## Build
Emit `meeting.deliver.resolved` on gate release/cancel/expire; `deliverHoldStore` subscribes and re-arms immediately; spoken deliver also sets `deliverHeld`. TTL becomes fallback only.
## Acceptance
Button un-holds instantly on resolve; spoken hold disables button; no gate-refused click; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-D: async KB rebuild off the HTTP request path
tasks.description: **Audit D-4 / KB-1 (High).** The KB service rebuilds the ENTIRE vault synchronously on the HTTP thread on every write (`server.ts:72,219,256` → sync fs in `builder.ts`). Blocks the loop; GETs stall — a latency spike exactly when a summary lands on the wall.
tasks.doneWhen: save-meeting returns promptly; a concurrent graph GET during a rebuild is not blocked (served from prior snapshot); rapid saves coalesce.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
Sync whole-vault rebuild on the HTTP thread on every save blocks the loop; GETs stall.
## Build
Respond first, rebuild async (worker/deferred); serve prior snapshot during rebuild; debounce rapid saves; keep last-good snapshot on failure.
## Acceptance
save-meeting fast; concurrent GET not blocked; saves coalesce; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-E: per-leg delivery reporting + email idempotency
tasks.description: **Audit D-5 / MTG-6 (Med).** `deliverMeetingSummary` runs email + KB independently but returns true only if both succeed; on email-OK/KB-fail it speaks 'didn't go through' though the email WAS sent, and a retry re-sends the email (no idempotency).
tasks.doneWhen: Email-OK/KB-fail speaks an accurate per-leg message (not 'didn't go through'); a retry does not re-send the email.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
Partial delivery reported as total failure; retry re-sends the email.
## Build
Return a structured per-leg result {email,kb}; speak per-leg outcome; make the email leg idempotent (dedupe key = date+title); retry skips already-sent legs.
## Acceptance
Accurate per-leg speech; no duplicate email on retry; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-F: poller resume jitter + generalized wall-busy quiesce
tasks.description: **Audit D-6/D-7, XC-2/3 (Med).** `poller.resume()` reschedules all 9 panels with no jitter → a synchronized burst (`poller.ts:150`). And task-151's freeze quiesce is proving-run-scoped only (`main.ts:921`); live briefings have the same contention with no pause.
tasks.doneWhen: Panels don't all refresh in the same tick on resume; a long live briefing pauses the poller like a proving run does.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
Resume fires a synchronized 9-panel burst; freeze quiesce only covers proving runs.
## Build
Add first-tick jitter on schedule; introduce a shared `wallBusy` gate (refcount) held by proving-run/briefing/meeting; poller yields while any hold is active.
## Acceptance
No synchronized burst; long briefing pauses poller; task-151 behavior intact; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-G: voice-turn deadline + spoken fallback
tasks.description: **Audit D-8 / XC-4 (Med).** The live `/voice/turn` sink awaits `sendAgentTurn` with no per-turn timeout (`main.ts:1160`). A hung gateway leaves the turn pending forever with no spoken feedback. task-152 protected only the proving-ground path.
tasks.doneWhen: An injected gateway stall produces a spoken fallback within the deadline; the pipeline accepts the next utterance afterward.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
No per-turn timeout on the live voice path; a hung gateway = silent forever-hang.
## Build
Wrap sendAgentTurn in a deadline; on breach speak a graceful fallback and unwind the turn (race-safe, no double-speak); mirror the task-152 watchdog.
## Acceptance
Injected stall → spoken fallback + clean unwind; normal turn unaffected; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-H: SSE idle timeout for research/search
tasks.description: **Audit D-10 / SR-1 (Med).** The Perplexity SSE read loop has no per-chunk idle timeout (`perplexity.ts:126`); a provider that opens then stalls keeps the run alive to the 120s hard cap — a 2-minute demo spinner.
tasks.doneWhen: A stalled SSE stream fails as ERROR well under 120s; a normal streaming response is unaffected.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
No idle timeout on the research SSE loop; a stalled stream spins to the 120s cap.
## Build
Add a per-chunk idle timeout (~12-20s, configurable) + optional first-byte timeout; end the run as `error` (view already renders it); preserve malformed-line skipping.
## Acceptance
Stalled stream fails fast as ERROR; steady stream completes; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-I: lift the HALT button clear of Vera's caption (operator-reported)
tasks.doneWhen: With Vera speaking a long (4+ line) caption during a live STEP item, the HALT button stays fully visible and clickable — the caption never overlaps it.
tasks.description: **Operator-reported (High).** During a live STEP run, Vera's spoken-caption overlay covers the HALT button so it can't be clicked while she speaks — forcing a wait for her to finish. task-135/RND-163 tried a fixed 88px clearance sized for a 3-line caption (`stepdock.css:213-217`), but longer captions (4+ lines) still cover it. A fixed magic-number clearance can't cover a variable-height caption.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
HALT button (in-flight `HaltBar`, `stepdock.css:213-217 .wr-pgdock--inflight`) still gets covered by Vera's caption on long (4+ line) responses; the prior 88px 3-line clearance is too small.
## Build
Make HALT clearance robust to ANY caption height: preferably measure the live `.wr-voice__caption` height at runtime (ResizeObserver -> CSS var) and sit HALT fully above it; or anchor the bar much higher up the wall + keep z-index above the voice overlay. Hit-area unchanged; STOP stays alongside; parked-dock position untouched.
## Acceptance
HALT visible+clickable against 1/3/5-line captions; still aborts the turn; render test asserts it clears the caption band; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-J: NEXT TEST ITEM preview block in the STEP dock (operator-requested)
tasks.doneWhen: While parked on the current item, the dock shows a purple, clearly-labeled 'NEXT TEST ITEM:' block at the bottom with the next item's id, title, and expected prose; correct on every step; clear 'no next' state on the last item.
tasks.description: **Operator-requested (High).** While conducting the current step, Jon needs to see WHAT the next test item is so he can get into the right screen/process before advancing. Today the parked dock (`StepDockBlock`, `StepDock.tsx`) only shows a tiny `NEXT: PG-0NN` id chip (~:355). It already receives `nextItemId` (:78/:97) and the full catalog is in `tab.records`, so the next item's title + expected can be looked up like the current item's record (:70).
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
Parked STEP dock shows only a tiny `NEXT: <id>` chip — not enough to prepare for the next item.
## Build
Add a new section at the BOTTOM of `StepDockBlock`: look up the next item via `tab.records.find(r=>r.item.id===nextItemId)`; render a PURPLE, clearly-labeled 'NEXT TEST ITEM:' block with the next item's id, title, and `expected` (pass-criteria = the 'description'). Handle `nextItemId===null` (last item) with a clear 'no next' state. Use a `--wr` purple/accent token, not a random hex. Subsume the old tiny chip.
## Acceptance
Purple 'NEXT TEST ITEM:' block at bottom shows next id+title+expected; visibly purple + distinct; last-item state clean; updates in place each step; render test; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-J1: Tier-2 nearest-command matcher (fast PTT path, no think)
tasks.doneWhen: A near-miss phrase for a registered command fires the correct HUD action with NO agent turn; ambiguous turns still fall through to Vera.
tasks.description: **Demo (High).** The `/voice/turn` sink (`bridge/src/main.ts` ~1140-1256) routes deterministically: briefing -> thinking -> range -> intents (`intents.dispatch` ~1180) -> panel-assign (`classifySpokenPanelAssign` ~1197) -> else fall through to slow `session.sendAgentTurn` (~1242). A near-miss phrase drops straight to the slow think today. Add a Tier-2 fuzzy matcher before the fallthrough.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
Near-miss command phrasing falls through to the slow agent turn instead of snapping to the known command.
## Build
New `bridge/src/command-match.ts`: deterministic offline fuzzy/alias scorer over `intents.listInfo()` phrases + panel-assign `VIEW_ALIASES`. In `voiceTurn` before `sendAgentTurn` (~1240): if best match >= tuned threshold, dispatch that canonical action (same rebroadcast+speak parity as Tier-1), no think; below threshold -> fall through. Tag result `tier2`.
## Acceptance
Near-miss snaps fast (no agent turn); ambiguous falls through (no false snaps); parity preserved; matcher precision/recall unit tests; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-J2: fast-first bias + per-turn latency instrumentation
tasks.doneWhen: Tier-1/Tier-2 turns log thinkMs=0; a plain fallthrough turn runs the FAST thinking level (never adaptive) unless deep is explicitly active; deep turn labeled think-deep.
tasks.description: **Demo (High).** `thinking.ts` (RND-89) already sets fast-default/deep-on-demand (wired main.ts ~758, applied ~1243). Missing: proof that Tier-1/2 commands paid ~0 think, and a guarantee the fallthrough never silently runs adaptive. Add fast-first assertion + per-turn latency stamp.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
No measurement proving fast commands are instant; fallthrough could run adaptive thinking.
## Build
Guarantee fallthrough carries explicit fast level unless deep active (audit `thinking.ts`+`main.ts` ~754-836,~1243). Add classifyMs/thinkMs + tier label (tier1-intent|tier1-panel|tier2-match|think-fast|think-deep) to `/voice/turn` result + one structured bridge log line/turn; Tier-1/2 must show thinkMs=0.
## Acceptance
Tier-1/2 thinkMs=0; plain turn = FAST level (test-asserted, never adaptive); deep labeled think-deep; thinking-level matrix unit test; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-J3: deep-think hero report in Briefing center column (panels 3/6/9)

Model a 3/6/9 tall hero report on it for deep-think answers; surrounding panels keep contextual numbers.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
Deep answers have no focused surface; Jon wants a tall 3/6/9 hero report in Briefing, numbers elsewhere.
## Build
Add a HUD preset making panels 3+6+9 one tall region; new `DeepAnswerView` (title+body, scrollable) registered in the view registry; bridge stages it on the DEEP path (task-166 think-deep) via snapshot/apply/panel.set/speak with glossary/search restore discipline. Fast/Tier-1/Tier-2 never trigger it. Don't regress briefing beat flow.
## Acceptance
Deep answer = tall 3/6/9 report in Briefing, others keep numbers; fast/T1/T2 don't stage; restores on dismiss/supersede; render+stage tests; gates green.

tasks.name: RND-J4: perdigones command glossary polish for the demo

tasks.name: RND-J5: demo warm-path preflight (no cold-start stall)
tasks.doneWhen: Running the preflight against live bridge+voice prints green PASS with latencies and leaves the session warm so the first real PTT command is not slower than steady-state; never arms test mode or touches the gateway.
tasks.description: **Demo (Med).** A fresh bridge/voice session is slow on the first turn (cold gateway session, poller, TTS, caches). The first PTT command in front of Zeri matters most. There is `/health` (18796) + a voice status preflight but no single demo warm-up command.
tasks.promptSummary:
## Problem
